intended_use: aBSI is intended to be used by trained healthcare professionals and researchers for acceptance, transfer, storage, image display, manipulation, quantification and reporting of digital medical images a
intended_use_full: aBSI is intended to be used by trained healthcare professionals and researchers for acceptance, transfer, storage, image display, manipulation, quantification and reporting of digital medical images acquired using nuclear medicine (NM) imaging. The device provides general Picture Archiving and Communications System (PACS) tools as well as a clinical application for oncology including lesion marking and quantitative analysis.
target_condition: Metastatic bone disease, specifically metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC)
ground_truth: Overall survival, symptomatic progression, and PCWG (Prostate Cancer Working Group) criteria for radiographic progression
other_metrics: Study I: aBSI significantly associated with overall survival (HR: 1.20; 95% CI: 1.14-1.26; P<0.001); in multivariate model (HR: 1.06; 95% CI: 1.01-1.11; P=0.03); associated with symptomatic progression (HR: 1.18; 95% CI: 1.13-1.23; P<0.001); associated with time to opiate use (HR: 1.21; 95% CI: 1.15-1.29; P<0.001). Median OS by aBSI quartile: 34.7, 27.3, 21.7, and 13.3 months. Study II: Kendall's Tau of 0.52 for aBSI increase association with OS; same Kendall's Tau (0.52) observed for time to radiographic bone progression compared to PCWG criteria.
